language-discrete-state-markov.claim_text: Discrete-state diffusion (D3PM, DiffusionLM) defines a forward Markov chain over discrete tokens — uniform / absorbing / Gaussian transition kernels — and trains a reverse denoising model that can generate fluent text without an autoregressive factorisation.

candidates.paper_title: Structured Denoising Diffusion Models in Discrete State-Spaces

useful_outcomes.rationale: D3PM defines uniform/absorbing/Gaussian discrete kernels and beats prior multinomial diffusion on text and image tokens, directly grounding the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides the canonical D3PM transition-kernel toolkit any practitioner can adopt today for discrete-token diffusion modelling.

plausible.rationale: Rigorously generalises multinomial diffusion with categorical Markov kernels and a sound discrete ELBO derivation.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on LM1B and CIFAR token grids; framework generalises but the paper itself stays at moderate scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports BPC/perplexity and FID baselines on standard text and image-token benchmarks that transfer cleanly.

candidates.paper_title: Diffusion-LM Improves Controllable Text Generation

useful_outcomes.rationale: Diffusion-LM is the named exemplar in the claim and shows controllable non-autoregressive text generation outperforming prior plug-and-play baselines on six control tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released training and control recipe lets practitioners apply continuous-embedding diffusion to controllable NLG immediately.

plausible.rationale: Continuous-embedding relaxation is principled but adds rounding noise versus a pure discrete Markov chain.

scalable.rationale: Trained at small (~80M) scale on E2E/ROC; scaling to LLM regime not demonstrated.

how_to_validate.rationale: Controllable-generation benchmark suite (syntax, semantics) provides a transferable evaluation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Argmax Flows and Multinomial Diffusion: Learning Categorical Distributions

useful_outcomes.rationale: Introduces multinomial diffusion that D3PM later generalises — a direct precursor demonstrating discrete categorical diffusion works.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Multinomial transition kernel is easy to drop into any categorical generative pipeline.

plausible.rationale: Derives a clean categorical ELBO with uniform-noise kernel; methodologically tight.

scalable.rationale: Validated on character-level text8 and image segmentation, not at modern LM scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports NLL/bits-per-character on text8 and standard categorical benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: DiffuSeq: Sequence to Sequence Text Generation with Diffusion Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Demonstrates non-autoregressive seq2seq text generation rivalling AR baselines on four conditional tasks, supporting the no-AR claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Partially-noising classifier-free seq2seq recipe is directly reusable for conditional text generation.

plausible.rationale: Builds on embedding-space diffusion with sound partial-noise objective; relies on continuous relaxation.

scalable.rationale: Encoder-decoder at modest scale; LLM-scale scaling not addressed.

how_to_validate.rationale: BLEU/ROUGE/BERTScore on paraphrase, dialogue, and QG tasks — standard transferable metrics.

candidates.paper_title: Analog Bits: Generating Discrete Data using Diffusion Models with Self-Conditioning

useful_outcomes.rationale: Generates discrete tokens via continuous bit diffusion; competitive on image-token and captioning tasks but a tangential route to the Markov-kernel claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Self-conditioning trick is a drop-in improvement many later discrete-diffusion systems adopt.

plausible.rationale: Binary-bit relaxation is simple and well-justified, though it sidesteps explicit discrete Markov kernels.

scalable.rationale: Shown on ImageNet token grids and captioning; scales cleanly to larger discrete vocabularies.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID and captioning BLEU give reasonable but not text-perplexity transferable validation.

candidates.paper_title: Step-unrolled Denoising Autoencoders for Text Generation

useful_outcomes.rationale: SUNDAE shows non-autoregressive iterative refinement reaches competitive MT BLEU, partially supporting non-AR discrete generation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Unrolled-denoising training recipe is reusable but predates the absorbing-kernel formulation in the claim.

plausible.rationale: Unrolled denoising is principled, but lacks the explicit forward-Markov-chain framing of D3PM.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on EN-DE WMT at moderate scale; no large-LM evidence.

how_to_validate.rationale: Standard MT BLEU and Python code generation — transferable evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: DiGress: Discrete Denoising diffusion for graph generation

useful_outcomes.rationale: Applies discrete-state denoising diffusion to graphs and beats baselines on molecule and graph benchmarks — supports the kernel framework, not text.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Discrete-kernel + transformer architecture transfers conceptually to token sequences.

plausible.rationale: Uses marginal-preserving discrete noise schedule with rigorous categorical ELBO.

scalable.rationale: Limited to graphs of <1k nodes; no language-scale demonstration.

how_to_validate.rationale: MMD and molecular-validity metrics — useful framework but not text-specific.

candidates.paper_title: DiffusER: Discrete Diffusion via Edit-based Reconstruction

useful_outcomes.rationale: Edit-based discrete diffusion reaches AR-level quality on machine translation and summarisation while enabling revision.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Edit-operation parameterisation is implementation-heavy and less directly reusable than absorbing kernels.

plausible.rationale: Insertions/deletions/substitutions form a valid but unconventional discrete Markov chain.

scalable.rationale: Small benchmark sizes; scaling behaviour and large-vocab efficiency unclear.

how_to_validate.rationale: BLEU/ROUGE on translation and summarisation provide a transferable but narrow validation.

candidates.paper_title: Muse: Text-To-Image Generation via Masked Generative Transformers

useful_outcomes.rationale: Masked-token generation over image tokens — adjacent to absorbing-kernel discrete diffusion but produces images, not fluent text.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Masking schedule and parallel-decoding recipe transfer to text-token diffusion modelling.

plausible.rationale: MaskGIT-style masked modelling is a degenerate absorbing-kernel discrete diffusion, methodologically sound.

scalable.rationale: Trained at 3B parameters on huge image-text corpora — strong evidence of scalability.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID on image generation gives no transferable text-perplexity validation strategy.

language-score-entropy-masked.claim_text: Score-entropy discrete diffusion (SEDD) and masked diffusion language models (MDLM) replace the divergence-based discrete-diffusion losses with score-entropy / masked-token objectives that scale more reliably and close the perplexity gap with autoregressive baselines.

candidates.paper_title: Discrete Diffusion Modeling by Estimating the Ratios of the Data Distribution

useful_outcomes.rationale: SEDD itself: score-entropy ratio-matching loss closes the perplexity gap and outperforms GPT-2 on zero-shot language modelling — directly grounds the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-source SEDD training recipe is the reference implementation any practitioner can adopt today.

plausible.rationale: Derives concrete-score / ratio-matching objective with rigorous theoretical grounding for discrete diffusion.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated up to GPT-2 medium scale with stable training and improved perplexity scaling.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports zero-shot perplexity across WikiText, LAMBADA, PTB — directly transferable AR-comparison protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Simple and Effective Masked Diffusion Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: MDLM is the named exemplar and shows masked-diffusion narrows the perplexity gap to AR on LM1B and OWT — direct claim support.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released MDLM recipe with weighted ELBO is a drop-in upgrade for any masked-diffusion language pipeline.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous absorbing-state ELBO with a weighted cross-entropy form that subsumes prior masked objectives.

scalable.rationale: Trained at GPT-2 medium scale; scaling laws shown but billion-parameter regime untested.

how_to_validate.rationale: OWT, LM1B perplexity and zero-shot transfer to several benchmarks — directly comparable to AR baselines.

candidates.paper_title: Simplified and Generalized Masked Diffusion for Discrete Data

useful_outcomes.rationale: Unifies masked-diffusion variants and reports SOTA perplexity among discrete-diffusion text models, supporting the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Clean Rao-Blackwellised loss and continuous-time formulation give an immediately reusable training recipe.

plausible.rationale: Provides a clean theoretical generalisation that subsumes SEDD/MDLM as special cases.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrates favourable scaling versus AR but caps at moderate model sizes.

how_to_validate.rationale: Perplexity on LM1B and OWT plus image-token benchmarks — solid but standard.

candidates.paper_title: DiffusionBERT: Improving Generative Masked Language Models with Diffusion Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Couples a BERT backbone with absorbing-state diffusion and improves generative perplexity, but does not yet close the AR gap.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Spindle noise schedule and BERT-init recipe are reusable engineering tricks for masked-diffusion practitioners.

plausible.rationale: Combines pretrained BERT with absorbing-kernel D3PM cleanly, though the loss is still divergence-based.

scalable.rationale: Limited to BERT-base scale, with no scaling-law analysis.

how_to_validate.rationale: Perplexity on LM1B and unconditional-generation quality offer partial validation.

candidates.paper_title: PepTune: De Novo Generation of Therapeutic Peptides with Multi-Objective-Guided Discrete Diffusion

useful_outcomes.rationale: Uses the MDLM framework to generate Pareto-optimal therapeutic peptides — demonstrates the masked objective scales to a new domain.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Adds Monte-Carlo tree guidance on top of MDLM, useful for conditional sampling but tangential to the loss claim.

plausible.rationale: Bond-dependent masking with MDLM ELBO is sound but specialised to peptide SMILES.

scalable.rationale: Restricted to peptide-sized vocabularies and sequences; no LM-scale evidence.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multi-objective peptide-property metrics, not transferable to perplexity-gap evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: Text-Guided Molecule Generation with Diffusion Language Model

useful_outcomes.rationale: Applies a diffusion language model to text-conditioned molecule SMILES generation; tangential to the score-entropy/masked-loss claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Domain-specific pipeline with limited transfer to general masked-diffusion LM practice.

plausible.rationale: Uses standard discrete diffusion without introducing score-entropy or rigorous masked-token loss.

scalable.rationale: Small SMILES corpus; no scaling analysis.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports MoleculeNet-style validity metrics, not perplexity-versus-AR.

candidates.paper_title: Generative latent diffusion language modeling yields anti-infective synthetic peptides

useful_outcomes.rationale: Latent diffusion on peptide sequences yields functional anti-infective hits but does not address score-entropy or masked-token objectives.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pipeline is peptide-specific and uses continuous latent rather than masked discrete loss.

plausible.rationale: Latent-space continuous diffusion is plausible but orthogonal to the discrete-loss claim.

scalable.rationale: Limited to short peptide sequences and a curated dataset.

how_to_validate.rationale: Wet-lab MIC assays give strong biological validation but no AR-perplexity comparison.

language-llm-scale-diffusion.claim_text: LLM-scale discrete diffusion models (LLaDA, DiffuLLaMA, BeyondAR) demonstrate that non-autoregressive diffusion can match — and in some regimes exceed — autoregressive LLM performance at billion-parameter scale, while enabling parallel decoding and bidirectional context.

candidates.paper_title: Large Language Diffusion Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: LLaDA is the named exemplar — 8B-parameter masked diffusion matches LLaMA-3 8B on MMLU/GSM8K and supports bidirectional reasoning.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released pretraining+SFT recipe and weights enable practitioners to deploy diffusion LLMs immediately.

plausible.rationale: Trained from scratch under standard pre-train + SFT paradigm with masked-token objective; rigorous comparison to AR.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated all the way to 8B parameters and 2.3T tokens — the strongest scaling evidence for the claim.

how_to_validate.rationale: Head-to-head on MMLU, GSM8K, HumanEval, reversal-curse — directly transferable AR-LLM benchmark suite.

candidates.paper_title: Scaling Diffusion Language Models via Adaptation from Autoregressive Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: DiffuLLaMA — the named exemplar — adapts GPT2/LLaMA up to 7B into diffusion LMs and matches AR on standard benchmarks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: AR-to-diffusion adaptation recipe lets practitioners reuse existing LLM weights and dramatically lowers training cost.

plausible.rationale: Continuous-time interpolation between AR and diffusion losses is theoretically clean and empirically validated.

scalable.rationale: Scales successfully to 7B parameters from existing LLaMA weights — explicit large-scale demonstration.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports LM perplexity and downstream zero-shot accuracy versus the parent AR model, transferable but narrower than LLaDA.

candidates.paper_title: Diffusion Language Models Can Perform Many Tasks with Scaling and Instruction-Finetuning

useful_outcomes.rationale: Shows diffusion LMs scaled and instruction-tuned solve diverse general-language tasks competitively with AR counterparts.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Demonstrates instruction-finetuning pipeline for diffusion LMs that practitioners can replicate.

plausible.rationale: Standard masked-diffusion training extended with instruction tuning; methodology is sound but incremental.

scalable.rationale: Scaling up to a few-billion parameters; below LLaDA's 8B regime but still LLM-scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports task-suite scores comparable to FLAN-style AR baselines — transferable validation.

candidates.paper_title: Likelihood-Based Diffusion Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Plaid 1B closes much of the likelihood gap with GPT-2 small and is released — early evidence that diffusion LMs can rival AR likelihoods.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Plaid 1B weights are public, but training recipe is more involved than later masked-diffusion approaches.

plausible.rationale: Tighter ELBO and noise-schedule analysis are principled and clearly improve likelihood.

scalable.rationale: Reaches 1B parameters — meaningful scale but well below modern LLM regimes.

how_to_validate.rationale: Standard language-modelling perplexity comparison versus GPT-2 — the cleanest possible AR-vs-diffusion test.

candidates.paper_title: Text Generation with Diffusion Language Models: A Pre-training Approach with Continuous Paragraph Denoise

useful_outcomes.rationale: GENIE pretrains a diffusion language model and improves over prior diffusion-LM baselines but does not match AR at scale.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Continuous-paragraph denoise pretraining is reusable but predates the masked-LLM-scale recipes.

plausible.rationale: Encoder-decoder diffusion with continuous-paragraph noise is plausible but not the strongest discrete-diffusion design.

scalable.rationale: Pretrained on 160GB corpus but architecture and parameter count remain sub-billion.

how_to_validate.rationale: Standard summarisation and QG BLEU/ROUGE provide partial validation against AR baselines.

candidates.paper_title: Empowering Diffusion Models on the Embedding Space for Text Generation

useful_outcomes.rationale: Difformer studies embedding-space optimisation issues but does not demonstrate LLM-scale parity with AR.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Diagnoses and fixes embedding-space training problems applicable to other diffusion-LM pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Analyses anisotropy and collapse problems in embedding diffusion — methodologically sound.

scalable.rationale: Experiments stay at small encoder-decoder scale; no large-LLM evidence.

how_to_validate.rationale: Conditional text-generation BLEU/ROUGE — standard but small-scale benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: PoetryDiffusion: Towards Joint Semantic and Metrical Manipulation in Poetry Generation

useful_outcomes.rationale: Targets controllable poetry rather than LLM-scale diffusion language modelling; tangential to the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Joint semantic/metric controller has narrow applicability beyond poetry generation.

plausible.rationale: Sound application of diffusion-LM to controlled generation but offers no scaling contribution.

scalable.rationale: Operates at very small model and data scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Poetry-specific automatic and human metrics — not transferable to AR-LLM comparison.
